user_query:
I’m spinning up a small internal initiative around agent-network bounty hunting and want a first pass that we can hand to the team.

Please do all of the following and keep everything file-based:

1) Read input/domains.csv for a list of domains (one per line). For each domain, run a discovery scan for AI-agent signals:
   - HTTP checks for these well-known paths: /.well-known/agent-protocol.json, /.well-known/agent.json, /.well-known/mcp.json, and /robots.txt
   - Basic HTTP response header scan for any x-agent-hub style indicators
   - DNS TXT check for _agent.<domain>
   Summarize findings per domain in a structured JSON file at output/scan/results.json. Each domain entry should include the domain, a checks object with keys for agent_protocol, agent_json, mcp_json, robots_txt, http_headers, and dns_txt (each with a status field like "found" or "missing" and any parsed metadata if available), and a discovered_signals list capturing any hubs or networks by name. Then write a human-readable report at output/scan/report.md that lists each domain with a brief summary of what was found.
   - If any discovered signals reference a bounty or task API (for example, a hub page or API that lists open tasks), include a bounties array in the corresponding domain’s JSON object with each bounty’s title and difficulty if you can extract them. Also add a short "Bounties" section to the markdown report summarizing what you found.

2) Read input/operation.txt (it describes a single on-chain operation related to one of the potential bounty flows). Produce a risk assessment at output/risk/assessment.txt including:
   - A clear Decision line with one of: APPROVE, CONDITIONAL_APPROVE, VETO, or HARD_VETO
   - Per-dimension ratings and notes for at least Slippage, Liquidity, Smart Contract, and Bridge (include Impermanent Loss if applicable), plus a short rationale

3) Read input/mortgage_params.json and calculate a concise Australian mortgage summary (assume 2024–25 settings) to help with staff bonus planning. Include LVR, estimated monthly principal-and-interest repayment, estimated stamp duty for the specified state/territory (with first-home concessions if applicable), an LMI estimate if LVR > 80%, and FHOG eligibility/amount. Write this to output/finance/mortgage_summary.md.

4) Create a minimal agent-first docs overlay we can drop into a repo under output/repo/:
   - output/repo/AGENTS.md that routes to docs/agent/index.md (keep AGENTS.md short and focused on navigation)
   - output/repo/docs/agent/index.md plus at least docs/agent/architecture.md and docs/agent/quality.md; each doc should have brief content and include frontmatter fields: title, purpose, owner, last_reviewed (ISO date), and source_of_truth
   - output/repo/scripts/agent_repo_check.py as a simple validator stub that would eventually check that required docs exist and have the frontmatter fields
   - output/repo/README.md explaining what’s in the overlay and how to run the validator stub

Notes and constraints:
- Treat input/domains.csv, input/operation.txt, and input/mortgage_params.json as the single sources of truth for inputs.
- Do not invent URL paths beyond those specified for discovery, but you may include best-effort parsed metadata when a file is "found".
- Keep everything self-contained and write only under output/ paths specified above.
